tone: Punchy, direct, charged. The current — short, charged, confident. Verbs first, numbers second, adjectives last. Volt is the loudest brand in the room without ever raising its voice into aggression. We are not wellness-soft, gym-bro, hustle-porn, or ironic.
do: Lead with verbs — pull, crack, lift, run, shift, finish.
Use numbers and nouns over adjectives (200mg, zero sugar).
Keep sentences short, declarative, and charged.
Stay specific — call out the can, the dose, the action.
dont: Don't make function claims (improves focus, boosts metabolism, replaces sleep).
Don't use macho or gym-bro language (beast mode, alpha, dominate, gains).
Don't lean on hustle clichés (rise and grind, hustle harder, unlock your potential).
Don't borrow Brisa's soft, mineral, sensory tone.
Don't co-lockup with the Onda parent on consumer surfaces.
promptFragments: high-contrast hard light, single key source
studio product shot, single can, sharp edge
metallic surface, anodized texture, no condensation
negativePromptFragments: no gym equipment or sweating athletes
no aggressive posing or explosion effects
no macho clichés or hustle-porn imagery
